steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: In the sentence “the toxins the organism uses to protect itself from predation actually ______ its attractiveness to predators,” the subject of the blank verb is “the toxins”—a plural noun.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a plural subject takes the base form of the verb (no -s/-es).
  • Choice A — "is increasing": singular present-progressive verb; disagrees with plural "the toxins." ✗
  • Choice B — "increase": base-form (plural) present-tense verb; agrees with plural "the toxins." ✓
  • Choice C — "increases": singular present-tense verb (third-person -s); disagrees with plural subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "has increased": singular present-perfect verb; disagrees with plural subject. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
